steps.title: Identify the modifier
steps.content: The phrase “Working with equipment designed for a billionths of a meter scale,” is a present‑participle clause that describes who is using the tools. For the sentence to be grammatical, the clause that follows must start with a noun (or noun phrase) that can logically perform that action.

steps.title: Match the subject to the modifier
steps.content:
Choice B begins with “nanoengineers,” the only option that names the people who can work with the equipment, giving a clear main clause: “nanoengineers have synthesized selenium nanoparticles …”.

Choices A, C, and D either make the nanoparticles the subject, use a possessive that breaks the link, or reorder the clause so the participial no longer modifies the actor.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
